title: Hygge, Coastal Cities & Nordic Charm
description: A cozy Scandinavian country of colorful harbors, cycling cities, royal castles, design culture, islands, seaside towns, and family-friendly attractions.

bestTime.description: Best for cycling, harbor life, beaches, islands, festivals, and long daylight evenings.
Good for city breaks, museums, castles, cafés, and fewer crowds.

highlights.title: Nyhavn & Copenhagen Canals
highlights.subtitle: Colorful Harbor Life
highlights.description: A historic waterfront district lined with colorful townhouses, wooden ships, cafés, and canal views.

highlights.title: Tivoli Gardens
highlights.subtitle: Historic Amusement Park
highlights.description: One of the world’s oldest amusement parks, known for gardens, vintage rides, lights, and fairytale atmosphere.

highlights.title: LEGO House & LEGOLAND Billund
highlights.subtitle: Creative Denmark
highlights.description: Billund celebrates the birthplace of LEGO with creative zones, brick masterpieces, and family attractions.

destinations.name: Copenhagen
destinations.description: Denmark’s capital is known for cycling culture, royal palaces, Nordic design, food, canals, and waterfront neighborhoods.

destinations.name: Nyhavn
destinations.description: A famous 17th-century harbor area with colorful buildings, wooden ships, cafés, and classic Copenhagen scenery.

destinations.name: Tivoli Gardens
destinations.description: A historic amusement park in Copenhagen known for gardens, lights, rides, performances, and fairytale charm.

destinations.name: Aarhus
destinations.description: Denmark’s second-largest city offers museums, creative neighborhoods, coastal views, food culture, and modern architecture.

destinations.name: Odense
destinations.description: A historic city associated with Hans Christian Andersen, with museums, cobbled streets, and Danish fairytale heritage.

destinations.name: Skagen
destinations.description: A northern seaside town where two seas meet, known for beaches, dunes, artists, and distinctive coastal light.

destinations.name: LEGOLAND Billund
destinations.description: A family attraction in the birthplace of LEGO, featuring themed rides, miniature worlds, and creative experiences.

destinations.name: LEGO House
destinations.description: An interactive creative experience in Billund dedicated to LEGO play, design, and imagination.

destinations.name: Rosenborg Castle
destinations.description: A Renaissance castle in Copenhagen housing royal collections, gardens, and Denmark’s crown jewels.

destinations.name: Kronborg Castle
destinations.description: A UNESCO-listed castle in Helsingør, famously associated with Shakespeare’s Hamlet.

destinations.name: Møns Klint
destinations.description: A dramatic stretch of white chalk cliffs overlooking the Baltic Sea, popular for nature walks and coastal views.

destinations.name: Ribe
destinations.description: Denmark’s oldest town, known for medieval streets, Viking heritage, historic houses, and cathedral views.

experiences.title: Spend an Evening at Tivoli Gardens
Visit Castles and Viking Heritage Sites

travelTips.title: Explore by bicycle
travelTips.text: Cycling is one of the easiest and most authentic ways to experience Danish cities.

travelTips.title: Pack a light jacket year-round
travelTips.text: Coastal winds can make the weather feel cooler than expected.

travelTips.title: Follow cycling etiquette
travelTips.text: Bike lanes are taken seriously, so learn basic cycling rules before riding.
